messageToReplyTo: Hi, I missed the deadline because I had a family issue this week. Is there any way I can still submit the reflection?
roughDraftReply: Dear student, I acknowledge your message. Late submissions are generally not accepted according to course policy. Please provide additional details for consideration.
audience: A student who missed a reflection deadline
purpose: Reply clearly while keeping the policy in mind
whatHappened: They had a family issue and asked whether they can still submit.
factsToPreserve: Late work policy matters. I can review the situation tomorrow.

messageToReplyTo: Can you tell me why Alex got a low participation mark this week?
roughDraftReply: Thank you for your inquiry. Participation marks are calculated based on observable classroom engagement and completion of assigned activities during the relevant period.
audience: A parent asking about a weekly participation mark
purpose: Explain the mark without sounding defensive
whatHappened: Alex missed two group activities and did not turn in the exit ticket.
factsToPreserve: Two missed group activities. Exit ticket not submitted. Invite a short call if helpful.

messageToReplyTo: We are still comparing vendors and may need another week.
roughDraftReply: Hello, following up on our previous communication. Please advise whether you would like to proceed with the proposal as discussed.
whatHappened: They need another week to compare vendors.
factsToPreserve: Offer to answer questions. Do not promise a discount.

messageToReplyTo: Sorry, I need to move tomorrow's demo. Can we do next week?
roughDraftReply: Thank you for the notification. We can accommodate your request and reschedule the demonstration for a mutually convenient time next week.
audience: A lead who needs to reschedule
whatHappened: They need to move tomorrow's demo to next week.
factsToPreserve: Ask for two times that work next week.

messageToReplyTo: Is the onboarding document ready for review?
roughDraftReply: I am writing to inform you that the document has been completed and is available for your review at your earliest convenience.
audience: A colleague waiting on a document
whatHappened: The document is ready, and there are margin notes where input would help.
factsToPreserve: Document ready. Margin notes need review.

messageToReplyTo: Can you send the numbers today?
roughDraftReply: Unfortunately, due to unforeseen circumstances, the requested numbers cannot be provided today. They will be shared when they are available.
whatHappened: The source file arrived late. I can send numbers by 10am tomorrow.
factsToPreserve: Source file arrived late. Send by 10am tomorrow.

messageToReplyTo: The export still looks wrong. Do you know what happened?
roughDraftReply: We apologize for any inconvenience caused. Our team is currently looking into the matter and will provide an update soon.
audience: A client reporting an export issue
purpose: Acknowledge and set a clear next update
whatHappened: The export job used last week's settings. I am checking the new settings now.
factsToPreserve: Do not promise it is fixed. Say I will send a clear update after checking.

messageToReplyTo: Why is this invoice higher than last month?
roughDraftReply: Thank you for contacting us regarding your invoice. The amount may vary based on service usage and applicable charges during the billing period.
audience: A customer asking about invoice amount
whatHappened: They added two seats on May 4. The higher amount includes the prorated seat charges.
factsToPreserve: Two seats added May 4. Includes prorated seat charges.
